description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ruby:2.5.0 Docker image and a postgres:alpine service, and defines the variables DATABASE_URL, POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_USER, and RAILS_ENV as shown. Set a global before_script that runs `set -xe`, downloads the Google Linux signing key, adds the Chrome apt source, updates apt and installs nodejs, libpq-dev, qt5-default, libqt5webkit5-dev, gstreamer1.0-plugins-base, gstreamer1.0-tools, gstreamer1.0-x, xvfb, libnss3, libgconf-2-4, and google-chrome-stable, then installs bundler via `gem install bundler --no-ri --no-rdoc` and runs `bundle install -j $(nproc) --path=cache/bundler --without production --jobs $(nproc) "${FLAGS[@]}"`. Define a cache named .cache_bundler keyed by the branch name that stores the path cache/bundler/ and is untracked, and attach this cache to the rspec job. In the test stage add an rspec job that executes `RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rails db:create db:schema:load`, `rspec --version`, `chromedriver -v`, and `xvfb-run -a bundle exec rspec`. The job uses the defined cache and runs on the default test stage with no additional rules or artifacts.
